It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1975.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1530 |title=向山古墳 |trans-title=Mukaiyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mukaiyama Kofun is located at the end of a low hill with an elevation of 50 meters on the south bank of the Kumozu River in central Mie Prefecture. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" type tumulus ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}). It is oriented east-to-west, with the front facing east, and has a total length of 82 meters. The tumulus was constructed in two tiers, and [[fukiishi]] and fragments of clay pottery have been found scattered around the mound, but no ''[[haniwa]]''. The [[burial chamber]] is located in the posterior portion and intersects the main axis of the tumulus at an angle. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by the local landlord in 1914 and was found to contain a clay coffin. [[Grave goods]], including three [[bronze mirror]]s, iron swords and spearheads, and tubular beads and wheel stones made from jasper, were discovered. These artifacts are stored at the [[Tokyo National Museum]] and are estimated to date from the latter half of the 4th century, in the early Kofun period. The tumulus is located about 10 minutes by car from [[Ise-Nakagawa Station]] on the [[ Kintetsu Osaka Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The surrounding area contains a number of large ''kofun'' from the same period, including the [[Takarazuka Kofun (Mie)|Takarazuka Kofun]], which are distributed around Mount Asaka.
A portion of the tumulus collapsed in 1973 due to the removal of topsoil, and it received protection as a National Historic Site in 1975.
*Overall length: 82.2 meters *Overall height: 10 meters *Posterior rectangular portion: 40.0 meters long x 46 meters wide x 6.0 meters high *Anterior circular portion: 35.4 meters long x 36.4 meters wide x 5.6 meters high
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Mie)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.matsusaka.mie.jp/site/culture-info/mukaiyamakohun.html Matsusaka city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=731047 Cultural properties of Mie Prefecture] {{in lang|ja}}
Mukaiyama Kofun

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Matsusaka, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Mukaiyama Kofun | native_name =向山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Mukaiyama Kofun (Matsusaka), setsumeiban.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mukaiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Mie Prefecture#Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mukaiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|36|42.98|N|136|28|11.53|E|region:JP-16|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Matsusaka, Mie]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 16,219 square meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Mukaiyama Kofun '''|向山古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in between the Ōno and the Ureshino-Ueno neighborhoods of the city of [[Matsusaka, Mie |Matsusaka]], [[Mie Prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1975.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1530 |title=向山古墳 |trans-title=Mukaiyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mukaiyama Kofun is located at the end of a low hill with an elevation of 50 meters on the south bank of the Kumozu River in central Mie Prefecture. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" type tumulus ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}). It is oriented east-to-west, with the front facing east, and has a total length of 82 meters. The tumulus was constructed in two tiers, and [[fukiishi]] and fragments of clay pottery have been found scattered around the mound, but no ''[[haniwa]]''. The [[burial chamber]] is located in the posterior portion and intersects the main axis of the tumulus at an angle. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by the local landlord in 1914 and was found to contain a clay coffin. [[Grave goods]], including three [[bronze mirror]]s, iron swords and spearheads, and tubular beads and wheel stones made from jasper, were discovered. These artifacts are stored at the [[Tokyo National Museum]] and are estimated to date from the latter half of the 4th century, in the early Kofun period. The tumulus is located about 10 minutes by car from [[Ise-Nakagawa Station]] on the [[ Kintetsu Osaka Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The surrounding area contains a number of large ''kofun'' from the same period, including the [[Takarazuka Kofun (Mie)|Takarazuka Kofun]], which are distributed around Mount Asaka.
A portion of the tumulus collapsed in 1973 due to the removal of topsoil, and it received protection as a National Historic Site in 1975.
*Overall length: 82.2 meters *Overall height: 10 meters *Posterior rectangular portion: 40.0 meters long x 46 meters wide x 6.0 meters high *Anterior circular portion: 35.4 meters long x 36.4 meters wide x 5.6 meters high
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Mie)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.matsusaka.mie.jp/site/culture-info/mukaiyamakohun.html Matsusaka city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=731047 Cultural properties of Mie Prefecture] {{in lang|ja}}
Mukaiyama Kofun

'''|向山古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in between the Ōno and the Ureshino-Ueno neighborhoods of the city of [[:en:Matsusaka, Mie |Matsusaka]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1975.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1530 |title=向山古墳 |trans-title=Mukaiyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mukaiyama Kofun is located at the end of a low hill with an elevation of 50 meters on the south bank of the Kumozu River in central Mie Prefecture. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" type tumulus ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}). It is oriented east-to-west, with the front facing east, and has a total length of 82 meters. The tumulus was constructed in two tiers, and [[fukiishi]] and fragments of clay pottery have been found scattered around the mound, but no ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is located in the posterior portion and intersects the main axis of the tumulus at an angle. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by the local landlord in 1914 and was found to contain a clay coffin. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], including three [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, iron swords and spearheads, and tubular beads and wheel stones made from jasper, were discovered. These artifacts are stored at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] and are estimated to date from the latter half of the 4th century, in the early Kofun period. The tumulus is located about 10 minutes by car from [[:en:Ise-Nakagawa Station|Ise-Nakagawa Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The surrounding area contains a number of large ''kofun'' from the same period, including the [[Takarazuka Kofun (Mie)|Takarazuka Kofun]], which are distributed around Mount Asaka.
A portion of the tumulus collapsed in 1973 due to the removal of topsoil, and it received protection as a National Historic Site in 1975.
*Overall length: 82.2 meters *Overall height: 10 meters *Posterior rectangular portion: 40.0 meters long x 46 meters wide x 6.0 meters high *Anterior circular portion: 35.4 meters long x 36.4 meters wide x 5.6 meters high

The tumulus is located about 10 minutes by car from [[:en:Ise-Nakagawa Station|Ise-Nakagawa Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The surrounding area contains a number of large ''kofun'' from the same period, including the [[Takarazuka Kofun (Mie)|Takarazuka Kofun]], which are distributed around Mount Asaka.
A portion of the tumulus collapsed in 1973 due to the removal of topsoil, and it received protection as a National Historic Site in 1975.

It is a "two conjoined rectangles" type tumulus ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}). It is oriented east-to-west, with the front facing east, and has a total length of 82 meters. The tumulus was constructed in two tiers, and [[fukiishi]] and fragments of clay pottery have been found scattered around the mound, but no ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is located in the posterior portion and intersects the main axis of the tumulus at an angle. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by the local landlord in 1914 and was found to contain a clay coffin. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], including three [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, iron swords and spearheads, and tubular beads and wheel stones made from jasper, were discovered. These artifacts are stored at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] and are estimated to date from the latter half of the 4th century, in the early Kofun period. The tumulus is located about 10 minutes by car from [[:en:Ise-Nakagawa Station|Ise-Nakagawa Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The surrounding area contains a number of large ''kofun'' from the same period, including the [[Takarazuka Kofun (Mie)|Takarazuka Kofun]], which are distributed around Mount Asaka.
A portion of the tumulus collapsed in 1973 due to the removal of topsoil, and it received protection as a National Historic Site in 1975.
*Overall length: 82.2 meters *Overall height: 10 meters *Posterior rectangular portion: 40.0 meters long x 46 meters wide x 6.0 meters high *Anterior circular portion: 35.4 meters long x 36.4 meters wide x 5.6 meters high

{{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Mukaiyama Kofun '''|向山古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in between the Ōno and the Ureshino-Ueno neighborhoods of the city of [[:en:Matsusaka, Mie |Matsusaka]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1975.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1530 |title=向山古墳 |trans-title=Mukaiyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mukaiyama Kofun is located at the end of a low hill with an elevation of 50 meters on the south bank of the Kumozu River in central Mie Prefecture. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" type tumulus ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}). It is oriented east-to-west, with the front facing east, and has a total length of 82 meters. The tumulus was constructed in two tiers, and [[fukiishi]] and fragments of clay pottery have been found scattered around the mound, but no ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is located in the posterior portion and intersects the main axis of the tumulus at an angle. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by the local landlord in 1914 and was found to contain a clay coffin. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], including three [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, iron swords and spearheads, and tubular beads and wheel stones made from jasper, were discovered. These artifacts are stored at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] and are estimated to date from the latter half of the 4th century, in the early Kofun period. The tumulus is located about 10 minutes by car from [[:en:Ise-Nakagawa Station|Ise-Nakagawa Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The surrounding area contains a number of large ''kofun'' from the same period, including the [[Takarazuka Kofun (Mie)|Takarazuka Kofun]], which are distributed around Mount Asaka.
A portion of the tumulus collapsed in 1973 due to the removal of topsoil, and it received protection as a National Historic Site in 1975.
*Overall length: 82.2 meters *Overall height: 10 meters *Posterior rectangular portion: 40.0 meters long x 46 meters wide x 6.0 meters high *Anterior circular portion: 35.4 meters long x 36.4 meters wide x 5.6 meters high

= 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.611939|N|136.469869|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Matsusaka, Mie|Matsusaka, Mie]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 16,219 square meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Mukaiyama Kofun '''|向山古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in between the Ōno and the Ureshino-Ueno neighborhoods of the city of [[:en:Matsusaka, Mie |Matsusaka]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1975.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1530 |title=向山古墳 |trans-title=Mukaiyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mukaiyama Kofun is located at the end of a low hill with an elevation of 50 meters on the south bank of the Kumozu River in central Mie Prefecture. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" type tumulus ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}). It is oriented east-to-west, with the front facing east, and has a total length of 82 meters. The tumulus was constructed in two tiers, and [[fukiishi]] and fragments of clay pottery have been found scattered around the mound, but no ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is located in the posterior portion and intersects the main axis of the tumulus at an angle. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by the local landlord in 1914 and was found to contain a clay coffin. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], including three [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, iron swords and spearheads, and tubular beads and wheel stones made from jasper, were discovered. These artifacts are stored at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] and are estimated to date from the latter half of the 4th century, in the early Kofun period. The tumulus is located about 10 minutes by car from [[:en:Ise-Nakagawa Station|Ise-Nakagawa Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The surrounding area contains a number of large ''kofun'' from the same period, including the [[Takarazuka Kofun (Mie)|Takarazuka Kofun]], which are distributed around Mount Asaka.
A portion of the tumulus collapsed in 1973 due to the removal of topsoil, and it received protection as a National Historic Site in 1975.
*Overall length: 82.2 meters *Overall height: 10 meters *Posterior rectangular portion: 40.0 meters long x 46 meters wide x 6.0 meters high *Anterior circular portion: 35.4 meters long x 36.4 meters wide x 5.6 meters high

It is a "two conjoined rectangles" type tumulus ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}). It is oriented east-to-west, with the front facing east, and has a total length of 82 meters. The tumulus was constructed in two tiers, and [[fukiishi]] and fragments of clay pottery have been found scattered around the mound, but no ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}''. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is located in the posterior portion and intersects the main axis of the tumulus at an angle. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by the local landlord in 1914 and was found to contain a clay coffin. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}}, including three {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}}s, iron swords and spearheads, and tubular beads and wheel stones made from jasper, were discovered. These artifacts are stored at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum}} and are estimated to date from the latter half of the 4th century, in the early Kofun period. The tumulus is located about 10 minutes by car from {{ill|Ise-Nakagawa Station|en|Ise-Nakagawa Station|WD=Q6079515|lt=Ise-Nakagawa Station}} on the [[:en:Kintetsu Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The surrounding area contains a number of large ''kofun'' from the same period, including the [[Takarazuka Kofun (Mie)|Takarazuka Kofun]], which are distributed around Mount Asaka.
A portion of the tumulus collapsed in 1973 due to the removal of topsoil, and it received protection as a National Historic Site in 1975.
*Overall length: 82.2 meters *Overall height: 10 meters *Posterior rectangular portion: 40.0 meters long x 46 meters wide x 6.0 meters high *Anterior circular portion: 35.4 meters long x 36.4 meters wide x 5.6 meters high

{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Matsusaka, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.611939|N|136.469869|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Matsusaka, Mie|Matsusaka, Mie]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 16,219 square = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Mukaiyama Kofun '''|向山古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in between the Ōno and the Ureshino-Ueno neighborhoods of the city of [[:en:Matsusaka, Mie |Matsusaka]], {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1975.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1530 |title=向山古墳 |trans-title=Mukaiyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mukaiyama Kofun is located at the end of a low hill with an elevation of 50 meters on the south bank of the Kumozu River in central Mie Prefecture. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" type tumulus ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}). It is oriented east-to-west, with the front facing east, and has a total length of 82 meters. The tumulus was constructed in two tiers, and [[fukiishi]] and fragments of clay pottery have been found scattered around the mound, but no ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}''. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is located in the posterior portion and intersects the main axis of the tumulus at an angle. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by the local landlord in 1914 and was found to contain a clay coffin. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}}, including three {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}}s, iron swords and spearheads, and tubular beads and wheel stones made from jasper, were discovered. These artifacts are stored at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum}} and are estimated to date from the latter half of the 4th century, in the early Kofun period. The tumulus is located about 10 minutes by car from {{ill|Ise-Nakagawa Station|en|Ise-Nakagawa Station|WD=Q6079515|lt=Ise-Nakagawa Station}} on the [[:en:Kintetsu Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The surrounding area contains a number of large ''kofun'' from the same period, including the [[Takarazuka Kofun (Mie)|Takarazuka Kofun]], which are distributed around Mount Asaka.
A portion of the tumulus collapsed in 1973 due to the removal of topsoil, and it received protection as a National Historic Site in 1975.
*Overall length: 82.2 meters *Overall height: 10 meters *Posterior rectangular portion: 40.0 meters long x 46 meters wide x 6.0 meters high *Anterior circular portion: 35.4 meters long x 36.4 meters wide x 5.6 meters high
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Mie)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Mie)|WD=Q20983066|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Mie)}}

It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1975.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1530 |title=向山古墳 |trans-title=Mukaiyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mukaiyama Kofun is located at the end of a low hill with an elevation of 50 meters on the south bank of the Kumozu River in central Mie Prefecture. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" type tumulus ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}). It is oriented east-to-west, with the front facing east, and has a total length of 82 meters. The tumulus was constructed in two tiers, and [[fukiishi]] and fragments of clay pottery have been found scattered around the mound, but no ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}''. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is located in the posterior portion and intersects the main axis of the tumulus at an angle. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by the local landlord in 1914 and was found to contain a clay coffin. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}}, including three {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}s, iron swords and spearheads, and tubular beads and wheel stones made from jasper, were discovered. These artifacts are stored at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum|1=Tokyo National Museum}} and are estimated to date from the latter half of the 4th century, in the early Kofun period. The tumulus is located about 10 minutes by car from {{ill|Ise-Nakagawa Station|en|Ise-Nakagawa Station|WD=Q6079515|lt=Ise-Nakagawa Station|1=Ise-Nakagawa Station}} on the [[:en:Kintetsu Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The surrounding area contains a number of large ''kofun'' from the same period, including the [[Takarazuka Kofun (Mie)|Takarazuka Kofun]], which are distributed around Mount Asaka.
A portion of the tumulus collapsed in 1973 due to the removal of topsoil, and it received protection as a National Historic Site in 1975.
*Overall length: 82.2 meters *Overall height: 10 meters *Posterior rectangular portion: 40.0 meters long x 46 meters wide x 6.0 meters high *Anterior circular portion: 35.4 meters long x 36.4 meters wide x 5.6 meters high
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Mie)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Mie)|WD=Q20983066|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Mie)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Mie)}}

The tumulus is located about 10 minutes by car from {{ill|Ise-Nakagawa Station|en|Ise-Nakagawa Station|qid=Q6079515|lt=Ise-Nakagawa Station|1=Ise-Nakagawa Station}} on the [[:en:Kintetsu Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The surrounding area contains a number of large ''kofun'' from the same period, including the [[Takarazuka Kofun (Mie)|Takarazuka Kofun]], which are distributed around Mount Asaka.
A portion of the tumulus collapsed in 1973 due to the removal of topsoil, and it received protection as a National Historic Site in 1975.
*Overall length: 82.2 meters *Overall height: 10 meters *Posterior rectangular portion: 40.0 meters long x 46 meters wide x 6.0 meters high *Anterior circular portion: 35.4 meters long x 36.4 meters wide x 5.6 meters high
